summary: Reis presents the results of a 1,101-response data engineering community survey, delivered as an interactive explorer rather than a traditional PDF report. Key findings reveal that while AI tool usage is nearly universal among individual practitioners, organizational AI adoption remains largely experimental, and data modeling continues to be a major pain point driven by organizational rather than technical challenges.
key_points: 82% of respondents use AI tools daily or more, but 64% of organizations are still only experimenting with AI
Data modeling remains a widespread struggle, with 59% citing pressure to move fast and only 11% saying it's going well
The biggest bottlenecks are organizational, not technical: legacy systems, lack of leadership direction, and poor requirements
Team outlook is cautiously optimistic with 42% expecting growth vs only 7% expecting shrinkage
Survey results delivered as an interactive explorer with SQL querying rather than a static PDF report
money_quotes: Rather than bury the results in a 30-page PDF behind a email form, I built something different: an interactive explorer where you can query the data yourself.
AI is table stakes. 82% of you use AI tools daily or more. Only 3.7% find them unhelpful. But organizational adoption lags way behind.
The bottlenecks aren't technical. Legacy systems top the list (25%), but lack of leadership direction (21%) and poor requirements (19%) are close behind. People problems rival tech debt.
key_insight: AI has become ubiquitous among individual data engineers but organizational adoption is stalled, and the field's biggest obstacles remain people problems — poor leadership direction, unclear requirements, and pressure to skip modeling — not technology.
